item.title: Flying Fijians complete preparations ahead of Wales Test
item.description: Preparations in camp have been completed by the FIJI Water Flying Fijians, with the squad now ready to take on Wales this Sunday. This was confirmed by Fiji Rugby Union Chairman John Sanday in an interview with fijivillage News this afternoon.
item.content:encoded:
Preparations in camp have been completed by the FIJI Water Flying Fijians, with the squad now ready to take on Wales this Sunday.

This was confirmed by Fiji Rugby Union Chairman John Sanday in an interview with fijivillage News this afternoon.

Sanday says the team has put in the necessary work during its preparations and is focused on delivering a strong performance against Wales.

FIJI Water Flying Fijians Acting Coach Senirusi Seruvakula says the team has pace and power and they will utilise this across the park for the full 80-minutes.

Fiji has beaten Wales twice with the most recent in Cardiff in November 2024, 24-19.

item.title: Switzerland advance to Round of 16 after 2-0 win over Algeria
item.description: Switzerland have booked their place in the Round of 16 of the FIFA World Cup after defeating Algeria 2-0 in their Round of 32 clash. Breel Embolo gave Switzerland the perfect start, finding the back of the net in the 10th minute to hand his side a 1-0 lead at halftime.
item.content:encoded:
Switzerland have booked their place in the Round of 16 of the FIFA World Cup after defeating Algeria 2-0 in their Round of 32 clash.

Breel Embolo gave Switzerland the perfect start, finding the back of the net in the 10th minute to hand his side a 1-0 lead at halftime.

Dan Ndoye doubled the advantage just a minute into the second half, scoring in the 46th minute as Switzerland took control of the match.

Algeria were unable to find a way back into the contest as Switzerland held on to secure the win and advance to the next round.

Switzerland will face the winner of the Colombia and Ghana clash in the Round of 16.

In other results, Portugal have booked their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 after coming from behind to beat Croatia 2-1 in a thrilling match.

They will now face European champions Spain at Dallas Stadium in Texas on Tuesday at 7am, after Spain also secured their place in the next round with a 3-0 win over Austria this morning.

In tomorrow’s Round of 32 matches, Australia will face Egypt at 6am, Cape Verde will face a stern test against Argentina at 10am, while Colombia will meet Ghana at 1.30pm.

item.title: Portugal edge Croatia to book Round of 16 clash with Spain
item.description: Portugal have booked their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 after coming from behind to beat Croatia 2-1 in a thrilling mat

item.title: Flying Fijians ready for Cardiff test with firepower across the park
item.description: FIJI Water Flying Fijian Coach Senirusi Seruvakula has selected a powerful and pace-filled 23-man squad as the team prepares to face Wales in their opening Nations Championship clash at Cardiff City Stadium on Sunday morning. Seruvakula says the squad blends physical strength, speed and experience, highlighted by the return of loose forward Peceli Yato and the inclusion of young tight-head prop Ti
item.content:encoded:
FIJI Water Flying Fijian Coach Senirusi Seruvakula has selected a powerful and pace-filled 23-man squad as the team prepares to face Wales in their opening Nations Championship clash at Cardiff City Stadium on Sunday morning.

Seruvakula says the squad blends physical strength, speed and experience, highlighted by the return of loose forward Peceli Yato and the inclusion of young tight-head prop Tim Hoyt.

He says every player had earned selection through consistent performances at club level and in recent campaigns.

Seruvakula praised Hoyt’s progress, noting that his form in France secured his opportunity following the unavailability of Luke Tagi.

The Coach says Hoyt has been showing good signs with his performances and numbers during his game time in France, and that provided him with this opportunity.

The midfield pairing of Josua Tuisova and Semi Radradra reunites, with Radradra returning to Test rugby after more than 18 months.

Jiuta Wainiqolo and Selestino Ravutaumada provide pace and finishing power on the wings, while Salesi Rayasi starts at fullback.

The bench provides depth and impact, with the return of Peceli Yato alongside experienced prop Peni Ravai and versatile back Vilimoni Botitu.

Seruvakula acknowledged the depth of talent available across both hemispheres made selection challenging but says the group is well prepared for the opening Test.

The Flying Fijians face a tough assignment against Wales in Cardiff, where a passionate home crowd is expected.

item.title: Goodman Fielder announced as Netball Fiji's new platinum sponsor
item.description: Netball Fiji has received a major boost after Goodman Fielder was announced as its new platinum sponsor to help develop the sport from the grassroots to the national level. The sponsorship was announced during a press conference attended by Goodman Fielder and Netball Fiji officials, new Fiji Pearls coach Michelle Parsons, members of the Fiji Pearls squad and representatives from the Fiji Sports C
item.content:encoded:
Netball Fiji has received a major boost after Goodman Fielder was announced as its new platinum sponsor to help develop the sport from the grassroots to the national level.

The sponsorship was announced during a press conference attended by Goodman Fielder and Netball Fiji officials, new Fiji Pearls coach Michelle Parsons, members of the Fiji Pearls squad and representatives from the Fiji Sports Council.

Goodman Fielder General Manager Tim Carter says the company is proud to support Netball Fiji and help the Fiji Pearls reach their full potential on the international stage.

He says the partnership also aims to encourage healthy lifestyles, empower women and girls, and help develop future leaders through sport.

Netball Fiji says the sponsorship comes at an important time as the sport continues to grow despite financial challenges.

The association says the funding will strengthen the pathway from school netball to the Fiji Pearls and give players better opportunities to prepare for international competition.

Fiji Pearls coach Michelle Parsons says the investment will allow the team to improve through better resources, equipment, recovery programmes and nutrition.

She says the Fiji Pearls hope to inspire more young girls to take up the sport and represent Fiji in the future.

Speaking on behalf of the Fiji Pearls, a team representative thanked Goodman Fielder for backing women's sport and believing in the team.

item.title: Spain beat Austria 3-0 to reach World Cup Round of 16
item.description: Spain have ended a 16-year wait for a FIFA World Cup knockout victory after beating Austria 3-0 in Los Angeles to book their place in the Round of 16. Spain controlled the match from the start and finally broke the deadlock in the 36th minute through Mikel Oyarzabal, giving them a 1-0 lead at the break.
item.content:encoded:
Spain have ended a 16-year wait for a FIFA World Cup knockout victory after beating Austria 3-0 in Los Angeles to book their place in the Round of 16.

Spain controlled the match from the start and finally broke the deadlock in the 36th minute through Mikel Oyarzabal, giving them a 1-0 lead at the break.

The European champions doubled their lead in the 66th minute through Pedro Porro before Oyarzabal sealed the victory in the 89th minute with his fourth goal of the tournament.

Meanwhile, Spain goalkeeper Unai Simón has set a new FIFA World Cup record for the longest clean sheet by a goalkeeper in World Cup finals matches, going 519 minutes without conceding a goal.

He breaks the previous record of 518 minutes set by Walter Zenga during the 1990 FIFA World Cup.

Spain will now face either Portugal or Croatia in the Round of 16.

Meanwhile, in other Round of 32 action, Portugal take on Croatia at 11am, while Switzerland meet Algeria at 3pm.

item.title: Nations Championship: Hoyt starts at tighthead, Yato on the bench
item.description: The FIJI Water Flying Fijians squad to take on Wales in their opening Nations Championship match has been named with young Lyon ti

item.title: Fijian focused head of crucial Wales clash in Cardiff
item.description: With just two days remaining before Fiji takes on Wales in Cardiff in the Nations Championship, excitement is building as the Flying Fijians prepare for one of their biggest tests of the international season. The squad has been putting the finishing touches on its preparations, with coaches and players focused on delivering a disciplined and physical performance against a Welsh side eager to impre
item.content:encoded:
With just two days remaining before Fiji takes on Wales in Cardiff in the Nations Championship, excitement is building as the Flying Fijians prepare for one of their biggest tests of the international season.

The squad has been putting the finishing touches on its preparations, with coaches and players focused on delivering a disciplined and physical performance against a Welsh side eager to impress on home soil.

The build-up to the match has also highlighted the strong rugby ties between Fiji and the United Kingdom.

British Deputy High Commissioner to Fiji, Michael Moody, recently reaffirmed the UK's support for rugby development in Fiji through a donation to the Fiji Rugby Union today.

Moody says rugby has a unique ability to bring people together, creating lasting connections and strengthening communities across Fiji.

He says the contribution is intended to support Fiji Rugby's grassroots programmes, ensuring the sport remains accessible to young players where future talent is identified and developed.

He added that the United Kingdom is proud to support initiatives that encourage greater participation in rugby and inspire the next generation of Flying Fijians.

Moody says as Fiji prepares to run onto the field in Cardiff, the continued support for rugby development serves as a reminder that the country's success on the international stage is built on strong grassroots foundations,with hopes that the next generation will continue to carry the Flying Fijians to greater heights.

item.title: Love to start at flyhalf and Savea to captain All Blacks against France
item.description: 25-year-old Ruben Love has forced his way into the All Blacks starting XV when they host France in Christchurch this Saturday.Love

item.description: The FIJI Water Flying Fijians are in full game-week mode in Cardiff, fine-tuning their preparations for Sunday's historic Nations

item.title: Belgium edge Senegal 3-2 to reach Round of 16
item.description: Belgium staged a remarkable comeback from two goals down to beat Senegal 3-2 after extra time and book their place in the FIFA Wor
